FUTURE ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Mar-09-2021 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #21, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar May-07-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Richard Collier for and with Peter Muhs (Trustee).

The Court noted that this matter is still filed under an estate case number and that they are dealing with a trust, and expressed concern about the amount of fees being requested. Mr. Muhs stated that this came out of substituted judgment in the conservatorship and that the case number was assigned by the Court. Mr. Collier stated that they will file the next accounting in a new trust case number, that the continuity and stability reflected in the twenty one year history is important to the Family, and that the fees are calculated on the same basis from time of inception at .75% of the market value and reduced by the investment fees. Mr. Collier also stated that the Trustee spends eight to ten days per month providing services and that he believes the .75% guideline fee is earned. The Court noted that fees were reduced in the last accounting period. The Twenty First Account of Trustee, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #21, REPORT, FEES

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #20, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jul-11-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Richard Collier for and with Peter Muhs (Trustee).

The Court noted that the Trustee's fees are high and at an hourly rate of $700.00. The parties explained services provided, time spent, Trust history, Mr. Muhs relationship with the Family, and Mr. Muhs experience as a Lawyer and an investor to support the Trustee's fee request. The Twentieth Account and Report of Trustee, etc. is taken under submission.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.
